name: Brita Lisqui Maxtra Pro Water Filter Jug
pros: Massive review count (8,954) proves reliable long-term performance in Japan
1.15L filtered capacity is practical for daily drinking and cooking
Removes PFAS chemicals—increasingly important for water safety concerns
cons: Cartridge replacement costs add up quickly (¥800-1,000 per cartridge)
Filter lifespan of 4 weeks is shorter than some competitors like Toray
shortDescription: Germany's trusted pitcher filter now optimized for Japanese tap water.
review: Brita's Lisqui is the safest choice for foreigners—widely available, English instructions, and proven effective against chlorine and sediment. The Maxtra Pro cartridges handle Japanese water's unique mineral content well. However, be prepared for ongoing cartridge costs; this is a subscription model masquerading as a one-time purchase.
